title: Floral simmer pot
subtitle: A rose petal and lavender stovetop simmer for scenting a room naturally
excerpt: A simmer pot releases fragrance by gently heating water with aromatic ingredients on the hob. This floral blend uses dried rose petals, lavender buds, and a little rose water for a soft, natural room scent. The pot can be topped up with water and reused several times before the fragrance fades. Not a recipe for a product to store; make it fresh and simmer for 1 to 2 hours.
sourceNotes: Stovetop simmer pots as a method of natural room fragrance: traditional home fragrance method. Aromatic release from dried botanicals via steam: standard aromatherapy reference. Never leave a simmer pot unattended or allow it to boil dry; a dry pot on a hot hob can scorch, char, and trigger a smoke alarm. Dried rose petals and lavender: food-grade if purchased from a culinary supplier.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Prepare the dry ingredient blend in a jar and store for up to 6 months; add water and rose water at time of simmering.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use the simmer pot fresh each time. The simmered liquid can be strained and used a second time within 24 hours, but the fragrance fades significantly on the second use. Discard after 24 hours.

glossaryTerms.definition: A pot of water with aromatic ingredients held at a gentle simmer on the hob to release fragrance into the room. The water must be kept at a low simmer, not a boil, and must never be allowed to evaporate completely.

content.text: is one of the simplest ways to fragrance a room using dried botanicals. As the water simmers gently, the aromatic compounds in the petals and buds volatilise and disperse through the air. The fragrance is lighter and shorter-lived than a candle or diffuser but entirely natural and free of synthetic fragrance compounds. This floral blend suits a living room or bedroom in spring. Keep the heat low and check the water level every 30 minutes; a pot that runs dry on a hot hob scorches.

attrs.title: Never leave unattended
attrs.body: A simmer pot must not be left on the hob while you are out of the house or room for extended periods. Check and top up with water every 30 minutes. If the pot boils dry, the residue scorches and smells unpleasant; on a gas hob there is a risk of the pan overheating.

items.prepNote: tap water; will be topped up during simmering
strengthens the rose note; food-grade or cosmetic grade

content.text: Combine the water, rose petals, lavender buds, and rose water in a small saucepan. Bring to a gentle simmer over a low heat; do not boil. Leave to simmer gently for 1 to 2 hours, checking and topping up the water level every 30 minutes to maintain the original level. The fragrance will be noticeable within 10 to 15 minutes.
Allow the pot to cool. The strained liquid can be stored in a jar in the refrigerator and reheated once within 24 hours. The fragrance will be gentler on the second use. Discard after 24 hours.
